advisory.title: /pair approve command path omitted caller scope subsetting and reopened device pairing escalation
advisory.description:
## Summary

The `/pair approve` command path called device approval without forwarding caller scopes into the core approval check.

## Impact

A caller that held pairing privileges but not admin privileges could approve a pending device request asking for broader scopes, including admin access.

## Affected Component

`extensions/device-pair/index.ts, src/infra/device-pairing.ts`

## Fixed Versions

- Affected: `<= 2026.3.24`
- Patched: `>= 2026.3.28`

## Fix

Fixed by commit `4ee4960de2330b5322127f925f3687dc6f105be1` (`Pairing: forward caller scopes during approval`).

introducingCommits.subject: Gateway/Plugins: device pairing + phone control plugins (#11755)

timeline.notes: The device-pair plugin introduces /pair approve and calls core approval without forwarding caller scopes. vulnerableHead stays on the last pre-fix snapshot immediately before 4ee4960de2330b5322127f925f3687dc6f105be1.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should detect brokenauthz when scanning vulnerableHead.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_is_fix_parent
checks.details: Using parent 2e23d44491c621e293925c8a624f6c50c1b762fe of fix commit 4ee4960de2330b5322127f925f3687dc6f105be1 as the last known vulnerable commit.
